T0365.title: Winner's Service Fee
T0365.definition: In gambling-related black market operations, 'kiln flower' refers to various fees forcibly taken from winners' profits, which is essentially a disguised commission for the house.
T0365.description: This money is typically collected under the guise of transportation fees, water fees, cigarette fees, or labor costs, and is deducted directly from the winner's earnings. This ensures the house profits regardless of the game's outcome. The percentage for the 'kiln flower' is often agreed upon in advance but can be increased on the spot, serving as a covert method to squeeze gamblers' profits. In mobile gambling setups, this fee also covers costs like transportation and lookouts, sustaining the entire illegal ecosystem.
